quotes.verbatim_quote:
if you take any 7th chord and lower the 3rd and 7th 1 fret each, you will
get a 9th chord built on the IV.
quotes.why_load_bearing: States the dominant→IV9 voice-leading transformation rule.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
You actually can take the 3rd and 57th of ANY dominant chord, and by lowering them | fret each,
you will get some form of a IV dominant chord
quotes.why_load_bearing: Generalizes the lowering transformation to any dominant.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
In any progression that has a series of dominant chords based on the cycle, you may progressively
lower the 3rd and 57th, one fret at a time.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Voice-leading rule through cycle-based dominant series.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
you may just use the 3rd and b7th of any dominant chord and play melodies on top for an
interesting effect.

For an interesting sounding resolution of V7 to I, you may try putting together any 3 notes of a
diminished 7th chord whose root is one fret higher than the root of the V7 chord, with the root
of the I chord.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Constructs the resolving diminished chord for V-I.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
This chord will now replace the V7 chord, and will be called a RESOLVING
DIMINISHED CHORD.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Names the technique central to Section 19.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Resolving diminished chords

also work well in place of m# type of chords, as long as the 7th tone of the m+is in the resolving dim.

chord.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Extends resolving-dim use to m(maj7) substitution.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
for a rock sound, quite often for a
major chord you can substitute the dominant 7th and its extensions

each measure the I'V chord of each basic chord is sandwiched between
two I chords.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Names the I-IV-I sandwich pattern in rock.

quotes.verbatim_quote: you may put in the IV of the IV before the IV.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Extends the sandwich principle by cycle-stepping.

quotes.verbatim_quote: When you do this, you are actually using the cycle in a clockwise motion.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Identifies the IV-stacking technique as clockwise cycle motion.
